channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump threatens to seize Kharg Island and other Iran oil infrastructure
item.description: Trump said the forthcoming operations to take over Iran's oil infrastructure, and its oil and gas markets, would be similar to U.S. actions in Venezuela.

item.title: Iran threatens Elon Musk's companies in Middle East: Iranian state media
item.description: Iran will consider all of Elon Musk's companies in the Middle East as military targets as it retaliates against the U.S., Iranian state media reported.

item.title: Oracle shares tumble 11% on increased capital raise, cash concerns
item.description: Oracle beat on earnings and revenue, but negative free cash flow and the company's plan to raise more capital is weighing on the stock.

item.title: Gold slumps to 6-month low even as inflation fears rise. Here's why bullion is out of favor
item.description: The yellow metal is at lowest level of the year as potential interest rate increases and faltering technical signals weigh on prices.

item.title: Josh Brown says a new AI trade is emerging and this 'Best Stock' is benefitting
item.description: Josh and Sean break down how another trade is taking the place of the chip stocks and other early AI trades — the AI customers.

item.title: Some Pentagon workers ordered to shelter in place as air quality issue is investigated
item.description: The Pentagon is the world's second-largest office building and is the workplace for thousands of military and civilian employees of the Defense Department.

item.title: As Trump sticks with Pulte, foreign surveillance program hurtles toward expiration
item.description: The U.S. House rejected a short-term extension of Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act. The surveillance authority expires Friday.

item.title: Elections advertising spend for 2026 expected to reach record high, outpacing presidential years
item.description: High-profile 2026 midterm races in California, Texas and other key states are driving higher spend earlier in the cycle, AdImpact found.

item.title: Bezos opens up about AI startup Prometheus after $12 billion raise: 'We're not being secretive'
item.description: Prometheus is Jeff Bezos' AI startup that launched in November with $6.2 billion in funding. Vik Bajaj is his co-CEO.

item.title: Wholesale prices rose 1.1% in May, more than expected, on surge in energy
item.description: The producer price index was expected to increase 0.7% in May, according to the Dow Jones consensus forecast.

item.title: SpaceX IPO won’t ‘break’ the bull market. But investors are worried about what comes next
item.description: As far as Wall Street is concerned, the stock market has what it takes to absorb the new supply.

item.title: SpaceX soon-to-be millionaires are set to spend big on luxury homes, watches and private jet travel
item.description: SpaceX employees have to wait to sell their shares, but many are already planning how to spend their windfalls.

item.title: Baldwin, Khanna propose new direct foreign investment review board to probe Trump's deals
item.description: Sen. Tammy Baldwin and Rep. Ro Khanna, both Democrats, raised concerns with President Donald Trump's courting of foreign investment.

item.title: DoorDash lets customers use photos, prompts to order food and book reservations in latest AI push
item.description: DoorDash wants to let users simplify the ordering and reservation process through AI.

item.title: SpaceX IPO is a ‘referendum’ on Musk, say market watchers
item.description: Friday's hotly anticipated SpaceX market debut could prove a defining moment for capital markets — and serve as a "referendum" on Elon Musk's leadership.

item.title: Wall Street sees one clear winner from the World Cup gambling boom
item.description: Several investment banks think DraftKings will benefit the most from the soccer tournament that kicks off Thursday.

item.title: Melania Trump announces a new savings account for youth in foster care
item.description: A new savings program for children in foster care was announced Thursday at an event with the first lady and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent

item.title: Trump might 'love the inflation,' but consumers are feeling the pain, experts say
item.description: For consumers, the Middle East conflict's impact on the cost of everyday goods has made it harder to make ends meet.

item.title: Lyft, Uber sue New York City to block driver retention law
item.description: Lyft joined Uber in suing New York City to block a new law they said would force them to keep bad drivers who threaten public and passenger safety.

item.title: Here are the odds of bear markets in each stock index this summer
item.description: For the S&P 500, a technical bear market decline of 20% from the closing high of 7,610 would mean a slide to 6,088.

item.title: Jim Cramer says this industrial stock is a buy ahead of its breakup this month
item.description: The Investing Club holds its "Morning Meeting" every weekday at 10:20 a.m. ET.

item.title: Food prices continue to rise. 8 money moves to cut costs at the grocery store
item.description: Food shopping can be expensive. Here are 8 ways to save money during your next grocery run.

item.title: As OpenAI leans into enterprise business, Apple and Google set sights on the masses
item.description: OpenAI's aggressive push into the enterprise, where it competes with Anthropic, coincides with efforts by Apple and Google to bring advanced AI to consumers.

item.title: Analysis: Trump said he loves inflation. Why that should be music to Kevin Warsh's ears
item.description: The president said Wednesday he is happy with inflation at 4.2%. That will be welcome news for new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh.

item.title: Supreme Court rules against private suits brought under key securities law
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with a group of investment funds affiliated with BlackRock and other asset managers to fend off lawsuits brought by private plaintiffs.

item.title: U.S. becomes India’s top gas supplier, as Iran war cuts it off from the Gulf
item.description: India's gas purchases from the U.S. have grown steadily, driven by Washington’s push to sell more American energy to the South Asian country.

item.title: ECB hikes interest rates for first time since 2023 as Iran war ramps up energy costs
item.description: The European Central Bank also raised its inflation forecasts and cut its growth outlook.

item.title: Mercedes-Benz joins Europe’s defense push with anti-drone vehicle deal
item.description: Mercedes becomes the latest European carmaker to turn to the defense industry in the hope that partnerships can revive the auto industry's ailing fortunes.

item.title: Beyond SpaceX: Here's where family offices see buying opportunities in the space economy
item.description: Personal investment firms of the ultra-rich are interested in space startups even without Elon Musk's name attached.

item.title: USDA's Rollins called screwworm a 'little pest' amid U.S. spread. Last year, she called it 'terrifying'
item.description: The U.S. agriculture secretary is downplaying the threat posed by the spreading screwworm now compared with her more dire remarks last year.
